raw_response:
Report B is the clear winner because it directly and courageously addresses the core implication of my question: "What are restaurants *actually* paying?" It doesn't just report on advertised salaries and official regulations; it dives into the prevalent "shadow economy" of cash-in-hand payments ("pod stołem"), which is the unspoken reality of the Polish gastronomy sector. This insight is far more valuable to me as an operator than a simple summary of job board data. Report B substantiates this bold claim with superior source diversity, citing specific economic reports (PIE), government classifications (*Barometr Zawodów*), and candid community forums (Reddit), lending it significant credibility. Its specificity, such as quoting data from a tipping app (TipTip) and providing a direct *net hourly rate* for cooks, makes the information more concrete and actionable. While Report A provides a solid, professional overview, Report B delivers the kind of ground-truth intelligence that allows an owner to understand the real market dynamics and make informed, albeit difficult, strategic decisions about compensation and hiring.
